kw.name: Safe Connect and Get Product Name
kw.doc: Setup: connecte et récupère le nom du produit sans faire échouer le test si la connexion échoue.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Connect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Get Device Information
kw.doc: Get the device information and return key specific device information

kw.name: Set Global Variable
kw.doc: Makes a variable available globally in all tests and suites.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Set Global Variable
kw.doc: Makes a variable available globally in all tests and suites.

kw.name: Text Screen
kw.doc: Get the current screen as JSON format of the corresponding device in the Response.

kw.name: Detect Screen Area
kw.doc: Set the screen area to the biggest element found in the current text screen (usually the largest window).

kw.name: Log Screen Area
kw.doc: Log the screen area in the report. Depending on the value of the ``log_as_image`` parameter, the area is logged as an image or as a string.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Connexion et récupération du nom du produit ignorées (terminal peut être indisponible)
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Connect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Disconnect
kw.doc: Stop connection of a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as disconnecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. This API will reboot the terminal if: - A package has been deleted during the Local Upgrade session - A file has been downloaded to the terminal during the Local Upgrade session (except when it's a web application)

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Disconnect
kw.doc: Stop connection of a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as disconnecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. This API will reboot the terminal if: - A package has been deleted during the Local Upgrade session - A file has been downloaded to the terminal during the Local Upgrade session (except when it's a web application)
kw.msg: HTTP request failed with status: 400 b'{"message":"Failed because NTPT3 was not connected in the first place","params":{"operation":"LOCAL UPGRADE DISCONNECT","device":"Move3500_54227826","peripheral":""},"code":-51}'

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Disconnect
kw.doc: Stop connection of a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as disconnecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. This API will reboot the terminal if: - A package has been deleted during the Local Upgrade session - A file has been downloaded to the terminal during the Local Upgrade session (except when it's a web application)
kw.msg: HTTP request failed with status: 400 b'{"message":"Failed because NTPT3 was not connected in the first place","params":{"operation":"LOCAL UPGRADE DISCONNECT","device":"Move3500_54227826","peripheral":""},"code":-51}'

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Connect
kw.doc: Start connection to perform a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as connecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Download
kw.doc: Download a file to the terminal. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as downloading a file with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. After downloading a file a reboot will occur after disconnection, except when downloading a web application to the following destination folder: /import/9993047031.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Disconnect
kw.doc: Stop connection of a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as disconnecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. This API will reboot the terminal if: - A package has been deleted during the Local Upgrade session - A file has been downloaded to the terminal during the Local Upgrade session (except when it's a web application)

kw.name: Wait For Terminal
kw.doc: Wait for the terminal to be available again.

kw.name: Disconnect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Safe Reset Screen
kw.doc: Teardown: revient à l'écran Payment sans faire échouer le test si le terminal n'est pas dans le bon état.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Press Keys With Delay
kw.doc: You can send one key or a list for keys with a specified delay between keys inside your body request.
kw.msg: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Press Keys With Delay' expected 4 arguments, got 2.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Wait For Text
kw.doc: Wait until the specified ``text`` is detected within the screen area.
kw.msg: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Wait For Text' expected 3 to 4 non-named arguments, got 2.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Find Text
kw.doc: Check if the screen within the area contains ``text`` and return the whole string that has matched. The parameter ``text`` matches the first label by substring or by python regular expression. For instance, imagine that ``Boot time = 31.25`` is displayed on the screen. Here are two ways to use *Find Text*
kw.msg: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Find Text' expected 3 to 4 non-named arguments, got 1.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Reset Screen ignoré (écran "Payment" non trouvé - terminal peut être déconnecté ou sur un autre écran)
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

test.name: Maintenance mode

kw.name: Pass Execution If
kw.arg: Skip test, not a Self Device
kw.doc: Conditionally skips rest of the current test, setup, or teardown with PASS status.
kw.msg: Execution passed with message: Skip test, not a Self Device

test.status: Skip test, not a Self Device

test.name: Install IFSF From Jenkins

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Télécharger IFSF depuis Jenkins puis définir répertoire
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Télécharger IFSF depuis Jenkins puis définir répertoire
Run Process
kw.doc: Runs a process and waits for it to complete.
kw.msg: Starting process: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "C:\Users\eslem.hajamor\Desktop\haj amor eslem\QAtcher_v3 (2)\QAtcher_v3\2.28.0\Test_Infrastructure\Test Infrastructure delivery-2.3.0-1\resources\sample\tests\download_ifsf_from_jenkins.ps1" -QAtcherTerminalID Move3500_54227826

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.
kw.msg: Échec du téléchargement IFSF: C:\Users\eslem.hajamor\Desktop\haj amor eslem\QAtcher_v3 (2)\QAtcher_v3\2.28.0\Test_Infrastructure\Test Infrastructure delivery-2.3.0-1\resources\sample\tests\download_ifsf_from_jenkins.ps1 : IFSF_JENKINS_URL doit Ãªtre dÃ©fini (variable d'environnement ou dans ifsf_config.ps1). + CategoryInfo : NotSpecified: (:) [Write-Error], WriteErrorException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.WriteErrorException,download_ifsf_from_jenkins.ps1 : 1 != 0

kw.name: Get Length
kw.doc: Returns and logs the length of the given item as an integer.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: msg=Aucune sortie du script de téléchargement
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Get From List
kw.doc: Returns the value specified with an ``index`` from ``list``.

kw.name: Strip String
kw.doc: Remove leading and/or trailing whitespaces from the given string.

kw.name: Set Suite Variable
kw.doc: Makes a variable available everywhere within the scope of the current suite.

kw.name: Log
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Should Not Be Empty
kw.arg: msg=Définir IFSF_BUILD_DIR (ou IFSF_JENKINS_URL dans l'environnement pour téléchargement auto)
kw.doc: Verifies that the given item is not empty.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Connect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Connect
kw.doc: Start connection to perform a local upgrade. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as connecting with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP.

kw.name: Continue For Loop If
kw.doc: Skips the current FOR loop iteration if the ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Local Upgrade Download
kw.doc: Download a file to the terminal. This uses the NTPT3 Protocol and has the same behavior as downloading a file with LLT. Please note that this API only works on Tetra terminals connected in USB/PPP or TCP. After downloading a file a reboot will occur after disconnection, except when downloading a web application to the following destination folder: /import/9993047031.

test.doc: Télécharge le dernier build IFSF depuis Jenkins (si IFSF_JENKINS_URL défini), prépare les fichiers selon le type de terminal (TETRA/T2/SELF), puis installe via Local Upgrade (/package et /import). Terminal type: IFSF_TERMINAL_TYPE ou déduit de QAtcherTerminalID.
test.status: Échec du téléchargement IFSF: C:\Users\eslem.hajamor\Desktop\haj amor eslem\QAtcher_v3 (2)\QAtcher_v3\2.28.0\Test_Infrastructure\Test Infrastructure delivery-2.3.0-1\resources\sample\tests\download_ifsf_from_jenkins.ps1 : IFSF_JENKINS_URL doit Ãªtre dÃ©fini (variable d'environnement ou dans ifsf_config.ps1). + CategoryInfo : NotSpecified: (:) [Write-Error], WriteErrorException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : Microsoft.PowerShell.Commands.WriteErrorException,download_ifsf_from_jenkins.ps1 : 1 != 0

test.name: Mettre le terminal en mode maintenance

kw.name: Connect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Set Maintenance Mode
kw.doc: Send true or false to the terminal to activate or deactivate the maintenance mode. After executing this API, the terminal will reboot. After 255 reboots the device automatically go back in exploitation mode.
kw.msg: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Set Maintenance Mode' expected 2 arguments, got 1.

kw.name: Wait For Terminal
kw.doc: Wait for the terminal to be available again.

kw.name: Log
kw.arg: Le terminal ${QAtcherTerminalID} est en mode maintenance.
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Safe Reset Screen
kw.doc: Teardown: revient à l'écran Payment sans faire échouer le test si le terminal n'est pas dans le bon état.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.doc: You can send one key or a list for keys with a specified delay between keys inside your body request.

The parameter ``text`` matches the first label by substring or by python regular expression. For instance, imagine that ``Boot time = 31.25`` is displayed on the screen. Here are two ways to use *Find Text*

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Reset Screen ignoré (écran "Payment" non trouvé - terminal peut être déconnecté ou sur un autre écran)
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

test.doc: Connecte le terminal, active le mode maintenance puis attend son redémarrage. Le terminal affichera l'écran MAINTENANCE. Ne déconnecte pas (terminal en maintenance).
test.status: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Set Maintenance Mode' expected 2 arguments, got 1.

test.name: Quitter le mode maintenance

kw.name: Connect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Set Maintenance Mode
kw.doc: Send true or false to the terminal to activate or deactivate the maintenance mode. After executing this API, the terminal will reboot. After 255 reboots the device automatically go back in exploitation mode.
kw.msg: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Set Maintenance Mode' expected 2 arguments, got 1.

kw.name: Wait For Terminal
kw.doc: Wait for the terminal to be available again.

kw.name: Disconnect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Log
kw.arg: Le terminal ${QAtcherTerminalID} a quitté le mode maintenance.
kw.doc: Logs the given message with the given level.

kw.name: Safe Reset Screen
kw.doc: Teardown: revient à l'écran Payment sans faire échouer le test si le terminal n'est pas dans le bon état.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.doc: You can send one key or a list for keys with a specified delay between keys inside your body request.

The parameter ``text`` matches the first label by substring or by python regular expression. For instance, imagine that ``Boot time = 31.25`` is displayed on the screen. kw.name: Run Keyword If

kw.name: Log

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Reset Screen ignoré (écran "Payment" non trouvé - terminal peut être déconnecté ou sur un autre écran)
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

test.doc: Désactive le mode maintenance du terminal. Le terminal redémarre et revient à l'écran normal.
test.status: Keyword 'QAtcherLibrary.Set Maintenance Mode' expected 2 arguments, got 1.

kw.name: Safe Disconnect Device
kw.doc: Teardown: déconnecte le device sans faire échouer le test si la déconnexion échoue.

kw.name: Run Keyword And Return Status
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with given arguments and returns the status as a Boolean value.

kw.name: Disconnect Device
kw.doc: Return OK or an error message

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.arg: Déconnexion du device ignorée (device peut être déjà déconnecté)
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.
